original_hypothesis.title: Guardian Approval Hash Excludes Proof Data Enabling Unauthorized Liveness Bond Return
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GuardianProver.approve() computes the approval hash as keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran)), excluding _proof entirely. The last guardian to trigger the threshold determines the actual proof.data passed to TaikoL1.proveBlock. A malicious guardian can include RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND (bytes32) in proof.data to trigger liveness bond return to the assigned prover, even though other guardians did not approve this action.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND causes transfer of livenessBond (250 TKO) to the assigned prover AND sets blk.livenessBond = 0. If the malicious guardian colludes with the assigned prover, this extracts 250 TKO per block that should have been slashed or retained.

proof_of_signal.pattern: hash = keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran)) - _proof is not included in approval hash

preconditions: At least one guardian is malicious or compromised
Malicious guardian is the one who triggers the minGuardians threshold (submits last)
Block's assigned prover would not normally deserve liveness bond return

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Malicious guardian crafts _proof.data = keccak256('RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND') in their approve() call, other guardians approved different proof.data
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: 250 TKO extracted per block via colluding with assigned prover: 250 TKO * N blocks

economic_questions: How many blocks could be affected?
What is the value of 250 TKO per block extraction?
Could the malicious guardian systematically target blocks where livenessBond should be slashed?
validation_requirements: Confirm proof.data is not part of guardian approval hash
Test that the last guardian's proof.data is what gets forwarded to TaikoL1
Verify RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND path triggers with guardian proof

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: 100% profit (no capital at risk for guardian)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any block where assigned prover failed to prove (should lose livenessBond)

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: 250 TKO per affected block * total blocks

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace guardian approval flow to confirm proof.data independence

validation_result.mechanism: Guardian approval hash excludes proof data
validation_result.impact_summary: A malicious guardian who triggers the minGuardians threshold can inject arbitrary proof.data (specifically RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND) that other guardians did not approve, causing 250 TKO liveness bond to be improperly returned to the assigned prover per block.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The GuardianProver.approve() function computes the consensus hash as keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran)) at line 46, completely excluding the _proof parameter. All guardians vote on this hash, but when the threshold is met, the last guardian's _proof is the one forwarded to TaikoL1.proveBlock(). In LibProving.proveBlock(), when the tier is the top tier (guardian tier, where contestBond == 0), there is a check for RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND in proof.data. If proof.data is exactly 32 bytes equal to keccak256('RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND'), the contract transfers blk.livenessBond to blk.assignedProver and sets blk.livenessBond = 0. A malicious guardian can craft their approve() call with _proof.data set to this magic value, while all other honest guardians submitted with different (or empty) proof.data. Since the hash doesn't cover proof data, the honest guardians' approvals still count toward the threshold, but the malicious guardian's proof.data is what gets executed.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code inspection confirms the vulnerability. GuardianProver.sol line 46 shows hash = keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran)) with no _proof inclusion. Line 50-51 shows the last guardian's _proof is forwarded directly to proveBlock. LibProving.sol lines ~167-173 show the RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND check that transfers livenessBond to assignedProver based solely on proof.data content, which the honest guardians never approved.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The approval hash on line 46 only includes _meta and _tran, completely excluding _proof. When threshold is met on line 48, the _proof from the triggering guardian (last to approve) is forwarded to TaikoL1.proveBlock on line 51. This means the last guardian unilaterally controls proof.data without other guardians' consent.

The RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND check triggers based on proof.data content. Since guardian tier is always the top tier (contestBond == 0), a malicious guardian can trigger this path by setting proof.data = keccak256('RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND'), causing 250 TKO (livenessBond) to be transferred to the assignedProver without other guardians' agreement.

calling_sequence: 1. Honest guardians call approve(_meta, _tran, _proof_honest) where _proof_honest.data is empty or normal
2. Each honest guardian's call computes hash = keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran)) and sets their approval bit
3. Malicious guardian waits until minGuardians-1 honest guardians have approved the same hash
4. Malicious guardian calls approve(_meta, _tran, _proof_malicious) where _proof_malicious.data = abi.encodePacked(RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND)
5. Same hash is computed (since _proof is excluded), threshold is met, approved_ = true
6. deleteApproval(hash) clears the approval, then ITaikoL1.proveBlock(_meta.id, abi.encode(_meta, _tran, _proof_malicious)) is called
7. TaikoL1.proveBlock decodes the input and calls LibProving.proveBlock with the malicious _proof
8. In LibProving, isTopTier is true for guardian tier, returnLivenessBond check passes, blk.livenessBond (250 TKO) is transferred to blk.assignedProver
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: GuardianProver.approve:46 computes hash without _proof → Guardians.approve:117 records approval bit → Guardians.isApproved:133 returns true at threshold → GuardianProver.approve:51 calls TaikoL1.proveBlock with malicious _proof → TaikoL1.proveBlock:82 decodes input → LibProving.proveBlock:167-173 checks and triggers RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND transfer

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration assumptions needed. The vulnerability is inherent in the code logic regardless of deployment parameters.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack is feasible with a single compromised guardian out of the minGuardians quorum. With 5 guardians and minGuardians=3, the attacker needs to be one of the 3 approving guardians. The attacker monitors the approval events, waits for 2 honest guardians to approve the same (meta, tran) hash, then submits their approval with crafted proof.data containing RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND. The attacker can collude with or be the assignedProver to extract 250 TKO per block. The attack is repeatable for every block where guardians are involved in proving.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Each successful attack extracts 250 TKO (the livenessBond) from the TaikoL1 contract to the assignedProver. In cases where the assignedProver should have had their bond slashed (e.g., they failed to prove within the proving window), this represents a direct economic loss to the protocol. Over time, this systematically drains TKO from the protocol treasury, potentially violating the solvency invariant (INV-ECON-001). Additionally, setting blk.livenessBond = 0 means the verification step in LibVerifying won't properly account for the bond in bondToReturn calculations.
recommended_mitigations: Include _proof (or at minimum _proof.data) in the guardian approval hash: hash = keccak256(abi.encode(_meta, _tran, _proof))
Alternatively, remove RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND from the guardian proof path entirely, or require it to be a separate explicit governance action
Add a check that all guardians who approved used the same proof.data by including it in the hash
Consider having each guardian explicitly signal whether RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND should be set, requiring threshold agreement on this specific action
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy TaikoL1, GuardianProver, and required dependencies in a test environment. 2. Set up 5 guardians with minGuardians=3. 3. Propose a block with an assigned prover and 250 TKO livenessBond. 4. Have 2 honest guardians call approve() with empty proof.data. 5. Have the 3rd guardian (attacker) call approve() with proof.data = abi.encodePacked(keccak256('RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND')). 6. Verify that the assignedProver received 250 TKO from TaikoL1. 7. Verify blk.livenessBond is now 0.
uncertainties: The exact timing constraints for the malicious guardian to be the threshold-triggering one - they need to observe and wait for other approvals
Whether the protocol's off-chain guardian coordination mechanisms provide any additional out-of-band verification of proof.data
additional_testing_needed: Verify the exact bytes encoding required for proof.data to match the RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND check (abi.encodePacked vs abi.encode for 32-byte value)
Test the interaction with LibVerifying.verifyBlocks after RETURN_LIVENESS_BOND is triggered to confirm bondToReturn calculations with zeroed livenessBond
Assess cumulative economic impact over many blocks
